steps.source:
use mod.tool
let request = workflow.input
let response_json = tool.call_json("math.add", {left: request.left, right: request.right})
response_json.parse_json()

use mod.std.array
use mod.std.assert
use mod.std.json
use mod.std.object
use mod.std.text
let request = workflow.input
let total = workflow.outputs.prepare.total
let route_key = "route"
let route = request[route_key]
let normalized_tags = []
for tag in array.reverse(request.tags) {
    array.push(normalized_tags, text.upper(tag))
}
let summary = object.merge({label: text.upper(text.trim(request.label)), route: route, total: total}, {tag_count: array.len(normalized_tags), tags: normalized_tags})
let fields = object.entries(summary)
assert(fields[0] == ["label", "RELEASE TOTAL"])
assert(fields[4] == ["tags", ["MATH", "RELEASE"]])
assert(normalized_tags == ["MATH", "RELEASE"])
json.parse(json.stringify(summary))
